name: host-orchestrator
description: Host-side parallel implementation + serial merge orchestration. Two slash commands: `/parallel-implement-wave` dispatches 2-6 GitHub issues in parallel using Claude Code's native subagent + worktree isolation (no Docker, no Sandcastle SDK, no OAuth token gymnastics); `/merge-orchestrate` runs intent-aware serial merge of 2-7 open PRs on host. Custom Opus subagents: `parallel-implementer` (TDD + vertical-slice with 7 anti-mock anti-patterns + bronze rule self-check) and `merge-resolver` (5 no-regression criteria). Host owns all remote mutations (push, gh pr create, gh pr merge); subagents are local-only by design. Sync parallel dispatch: 1 host message with N Agent calls in parallel, blocking until all return. Audit log append-only in `.host-orchestrator/waves/<ts>.log`. Smart-wave retry for crashed/stuck/push-failed agents. Complementary to sandcastle-max (Docker AFK, larger batches). Renamed from `merge-orchestrator` v0.1.0; v2.0.0 adds `/parallel-implement-wave` and removes the auto-invocable skill (slash commands only — explicit invocation for heavy actions).
